revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「猿田彦神社（さるたひこじんじゃ）は、福井県福井市冬野町にある神社。旧社格は村社。 歴史 この神社は、廃仏毀釈以前には蕗野寺（吹野寺・冬野寺）と呼ばれる寺院であった。 神社に伝わる縁起額によると、大宝年間（701ー703）に泰澄開基となった三番目の寺であるという。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.24</ref>。…」
revision.text:
猿田彦神社（さるたひこじんじゃ）は、福井県福井市冬野町にある神社。旧社格は村社。
歴史 この神社は、廃仏毀釈以前には蕗野寺（吹野寺・冬野寺）と呼ばれる寺院であった。
神社に伝わる縁起額によると、大宝年間（701ー703）に泰澄開基となった三番目の寺であるという。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.24</ref>。
境内 雨乞いの池
祭神 [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] 白髭命 十一面観音菩薩像（福井県指定の文化財）<ref>福井市の文化財 福井県教育委員会編 </ref>
祭礼 祭礼は、毎年春に行われる。 かつては、４月１３日から１４日にかけて行われていたが、現在はその日程に近い土日に行われる（令和５年（2023）は4月8日、9日だった）。 祭礼では、猿田彦神社から「ヤド（宿）」となる家へ、大きな御幣をつけられた「オテングサン（お天狗さん）と言われる「天狗面」、「オシシサン（お獅子さん）」と呼ばれる「獅子頭」、御幣のつけられた鉾、御幣、膳が運ばれ、ヤドの床の間に置かれて一泊された後、戻られる。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.32−33</ref>。
参考文献[ソースを編集] 泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 平成２４年

猿田彦神社（さるたひこじんじゃ）は、福井県福井市冬野町にある神社。旧社格は村社。
歴史
この神社は、廃仏毀釈以前には蕗野寺（吹野寺・冬野寺）と呼ばれる寺院であった。
神社に伝わる縁起額によると、大宝年間（701ー703）に泰澄開基となった三番目の寺であるという。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.24</ref>。
境内
雨乞いの池
祭神
[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]
白髭命
十一面観音菩薩像（福井県指定の文化財）<ref>福井市の文化財 福井県教育委員会編 </ref>
祭礼
祭礼は、毎年春に行われる。 かつては、４月１３日から１４日にかけて行われていたが、現在はその日程に近い土日に行われる（令和５年（2023）は4月8日、9日だった）。 祭礼では、猿田彦神社から「ヤド（宿）」となる家へ、大きな御幣をつけられた「オテングサン（お天狗さん）と言われる「天狗面」、「オシシサン（お獅子さん）」と呼ばれる「獅子頭」、御幣のつけられた鉾、御幣、膳が運ばれ、ヤドの床の間に置かれて一泊された後、戻られる。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.32−33</ref>。
参考文献[ソースを編集]
泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 平成２４年

この神社は、廃仏毀釈以前には蕗野寺（吹野寺・冬野寺）と呼ばれる寺院であった。
神社に伝わる縁起額によると、大宝年間（701ー703）に泰澄開基となった三番目の寺であるという。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.24</ref>。

[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]
白髭命
十一面観音菩薩像（福井県指定の文化財）<ref>福井市の文化財 福井県教育委員会編 </ref>

祭礼は、毎年春に行われる。 かつては、４月１３日から１４日にかけて行われていたが、現在はその日程に近い土日に行われる（令和５年（2023）は4月8日、9日だった）。 祭礼では、猿田彦神社から「ヤド（宿）」となる家へ、大きな御幣をつけられた「オテングサン（お天狗さん）と言われる「天狗面」、「オシシサン（お獅子さん）」と呼ばれる「獅子頭」、御幣のつけられた鉾、御幣、膳が運ばれ、ヤドの床の間に置かれて一泊された後、戻られる。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.32−33</ref>。

神社に伝わる縁起額によると、[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]年間（701ー704）に[[泰澄]]開基となった三番目の寺であるという。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.24</ref>。
== 境内 ==
雨乞いの池
== 祭神 ==
[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]
白髭命
十一面観音菩薩像（福井県指定の文化財）<ref>福井市の文化財 福井県教育委員会編 p.80</ref>
== 祭礼 ==
祭礼は、毎年春に行われる。 かつては、４月１３日から１４日にかけて行われていたが、現在はその日程に近い土日に行われる（令和５年（2023）は4月8日、9日だった）。 祭礼では、猿田彦神社から「ヤド（宿）」となる家へ、大きな[[御幣]]をつけられた「オテングサン（お天狗さん）と言われる「天狗面」、「オシシサン（お獅子さん）」と呼ばれる「獅子頭」、御幣のつけられた鉾、御幣、膳が運ばれ、ヤドの床の間に置かれて一泊された後、戻られる。<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.32−33</ref>。
== 参考文献 ==
『泰澄ゆかりの神仏』福井県立歴史博物館編 2012年
『福井市の文化財』福井市教育委員会、2021年

* [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] * 白髭命 * 十一面観音菩薩像（福井県指定の文化財）<ref>福井市の文化財 福井市教育委員会編 p.80</ref>
== 祭礼 == 祭礼は、毎年春に行われる。かつては、4月13日から4月14日にかけて行われていたが、現在はその日程に近い土日に行われる（2023年〈令和5年〉は4月8日、4月9日であった）。祭礼では、猿田彦神社から「ヤド（宿）」となる家へ、大きな[[御幣]]をつけた「オテングサン（お天狗さん）と言われる「天狗面」、「オシシサン（お獅子さん）」と呼ばれる「獅子頭」、御幣のつけられた鉾、御幣、膳が運ばれ、ヤドの床の間に置かれて一泊した後、戻る<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.32−33</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references /> == 参考文献 ==
* 『泰澄ゆかりの神仏』福井県立歴史博物館編 2012年 * 『福井市の文化財』福井市教育委員会編 2021年
== 外部リンク ==
* [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 福井県神社庁]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さるたひこじんじゃ}} [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:福井市の建築物]] [[Category:福井市の歴史]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:8世紀設立]]

{{神社 |名称=猿田彦神社 | 画像= {{画像募集中|cat=福井市}} |所在地=[[福井県]][[福井市]]冬野町29-2 | 緯度度 =36 | 緯度分 =00 | 緯度秒 =47.23 | 経度度 =136 | 経度分 =11 | 経度秒 =13.73 |地図国コード = JP |祭神=[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]、白髭命、十一面観音菩薩 |神体= |社格= |創建=[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]年間 |本殿= （不明） |例祭=4月 |OSMサイズ = |OSMズーム = }} '''猿田彦神社'''（さるたひこじんじゃ）は、福井県[[福井市]]冬野町にある神社。
[[城山 (福井市)|城山（福井市）]]の麓。[[旧社格]]は村社。
== 歴史 == この神社は、[[廃仏毀釈]]以前には蕗野寺（吹野寺・冬野寺）と呼ばれる寺院であった。
神社に伝わる縁起額によると、[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]年間（701年 - 704年）に[[泰澄]]開基となった三番目の寺であるという<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.24</ref>。
== 境内 ==
* 雨乞いの池
== 祭神 ==
* [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] * 白髭命 * 十一面観音菩薩像（福井県指定の文化財）<ref>福井市の文化財 福井市教育委員会編 p.80</ref>
== 祭礼 == 祭礼は、毎年春に行われる。かつては、4月13日から4月14日にかけて行われていたが、現在はその日程に近い土日に行われる（2023年〈令和5年〉は4月8日、4月9日であった）。祭礼では、猿田彦神社から「ヤド（宿）」となる家へ、大きな[[御幣]]をつけた「オテングサン（お天狗さん）と言われる「天狗面」、「オシシサン（お獅子さん）」と呼ばれる「獅子頭」、御幣のつけられた鉾、御幣、膳が運ばれ、ヤドの床の間に置かれて一泊した後、戻る<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.32−33</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references /> == 参考文献 ==
* 『泰澄ゆかりの神仏』福井県立歴史博物館編 2012年 * 『福井市の文化財』福井市教育委員会編 2021年
== 外部リンク ==
* [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 福井県神社庁]

revision.text: [[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:qq--qq]]

[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:qq--qq]] {{Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}, {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市}}, Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命}}, {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音菩薩}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_rank= |established= {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝 (日本)}} (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= {{ill|April|ja|4月}} }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}, at the base of **{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)}}**. It was formerly classified as a **village shrine** (村社).
== 歴史 == This shrine was originally a **Buddhist temple** known as **Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)** before the **Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation** (**{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}}**).
According to the **enshrinement plaque** preserved at the shrine, it was the **third temple** founded by **{{ill|Taichō|ja|泰澄}}** during the **Taihō era** (701–704 CE).<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== 境内 == * **Rain-calling Pond** (雨乞いの池)
== 祭神 == * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命}} * **Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva** (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a **Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture**.<ref>**"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== 祭礼 == The **annual festival** is held **in spring** every year. Previously, it was observed on **April 13–14**, but it is now celebrated **on the closest weekend** (for example, in **2023**, it was held on **April 8–9**).
During the festival, **sacred objects** are carried **from the shrine** to a designated **host home (Yado 宿)**. These include: - **"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)** – a **Tengu mask** (天狗面) - **"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)** – a **lion head** (獅子頭) - **A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)** - **Gohei (御幣)** - **Sacred offerings (zen 膳)**
These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== 脚注 == <references />
== 参考文献 == * **"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * **"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukui]] [[Category:History of Fukui]] [[Category:Village shrines]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]]

== 祭礼 == The **annual festival** is held **in spring** every year. Previously, it was observed on **April 13–14**, but it is now celebrated **on the closest weekend** (for example, in **2023**, it was held on **April 8–9**).
During the festival, **sacred objects** are carried **from the shrine** to a designated **host home (Yado 宿)**. These include: - **"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)** – a **Tengu mask** (天狗面) - **"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)** – a **lion head** (獅子頭) - **A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)** - **Gohei (御幣)** - **Sacred offerings (zen 膳)**
These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>

[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:qq--qq]] {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}, {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市}}, Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命}}, {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音菩薩}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_rank= |established= {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝 (日本)}} (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= {{ill|April|ja|4月}} }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}, at the base of **{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)}}**. It was formerly classified as a **village shrine** (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a **Buddhist temple** known as **Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)** before the **Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation** (**{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}}**).
According to the **enshrinement plaque** preserved at the shrine, it was the **third temple** founded by **{{ill|Taichō|ja|泰澄}}** during the **Taihō era** (701–704 CE).<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== 境内 == * **Rain-calling Pond** (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命}} * **Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva** (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a **Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture**.<ref>**"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== 祭礼 == The **annual festival** is held **in spring** every year. Previously, it was observed on **April 13–14**, but it is now celebrated **on the closest weekend** (for example, in **2023**, it was held on **April 8–9**).
During the festival, **sacred objects** are carried **from the shrine** to a designated **host home (Yado 宿)**. These include: - **"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)** – a **Tengu mask** (天狗面) - **"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)** – a **lion head** (獅子頭) - **A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)** - **Gohei (御幣)** - **Sacred offerings (zen 膳)**
These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== 脚注 == <references />
== 参考文献 == * **"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * **"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.

== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukui]] [[Category:History of Fukui]] [[Category:Village shrines]] [[Category:8th-century establishments in Japan]] [[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== 境内" to "== Shrine facilities"
revision.text:
[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:New-]] {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}, {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市}}, Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命}}, {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音菩薩}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_rank= |established= {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝 (日本)}} (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= {{ill|April|ja|4月}} }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}, at the base of **{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)}}**. It was formerly classified as a **village shrine** (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a **Buddhist temple** known as **Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)** before the **Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation** (**{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}}**).
According to the **enshrinement plaque** preserved at the shrine, it was the **third temple** founded by **{{ill|Taichō|ja|泰澄}}** during the **Taihō era** (701–704 CE).<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * **Rain-calling Pond** (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命}} * **Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva** (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a **Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture**.<ref>**"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== 祭礼 == The **annual festival** is held **in spring** every year. Previously, it was observed on **April 13–14**, but it is now celebrated **on the closest weekend** (for example, in **2023**, it was held on **April 8–9**).
During the festival, **sacred objects** are carried **from the shrine** to a designated **host home (Yado 宿)**. These include: - **"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)** – a **Tengu mask** (天狗面) - **"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)** – a **lion head** (獅子頭) - **A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)** - **Gohei (御幣)** - **Sacred offerings (zen 膳)**
These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== 参考文献 == * **"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * **"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市|12=draft}}, Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音菩薩|12=draft}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_rank= |established= {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝 (日本)|12=draft}} (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= {{ill|April|ja|4月|12=draft}} }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|12=draft}}, at the base of **{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|12=draft}}**. It was formerly classified as a **village shrine** (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a **Buddhist temple** known as **Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)** before the **Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation** (**{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}}**).
According to the **enshrinement plaque** preserved at the shrine, it was the **third temple** founded by **{{ill|Taichō|ja|泰澄|12=draft}}** during the **Taihō era** (701–704 CE).<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * **Rain-calling Pond** (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=draft}} * **Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva** (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a **Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture**.<ref>**"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The **annual festival** is held **in spring** every year. Previously, it was observed on **April 13–14**, but it is now celebrated **on the closest weekend** (for example, in **2023**, it was held on **April 8–9**).
During the festival, **sacred objects** are carried **from the shrine** to a designated **host home (Yado 宿)**. These include: - **"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)** – a **Tengu mask** (天狗面) - **"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)** – a **lion head** (獅子頭) - **A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)** - **Gohei (御幣)** - **Sacred offerings (zen 膳)**
These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== 参考文献 == * **"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * **"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== 参考文献 == * **"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * **"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.

[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] [[sn:Sarutahiko Shrine (Fukui)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "rank" to "Rank"
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= |established= {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= {{ill|April|ja|4月|12=draft|12=draft}} }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|12=draft|12=draft}}, at the base of **{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|12=draft|12=draft}}**. It was formerly classified as a **village shrine** (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a **Buddhist temple** known as **Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)** before the **Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation** (**{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft|12=draft}}**).
According to the **enshrinement plaque** preserved at the shrine, it was the **third temple** founded by **{{ill|Taichō|ja|泰澄|12=draft|12=draft}}** during the **Taihō era** (701–704 CE).<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * **Rain-calling Pond** (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=draft|12=draft}} * **Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva** (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a **Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture**.<ref>**"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The **annual festival** is held **in spring** every year. Previously, it was observed on **April 13–14**, but it is now celebrated **on the closest weekend** (for example, in **2023**, it was held on **April 8–9**).
During the festival, **sacred objects** are carried **from the shrine** to a designated **host home (Yado 宿)**. These include: - **"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)** – a **Tengu mask** (天狗面) - **"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)** – a **lion head** (獅子頭) - **A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)** - **Gohei (御幣)** - **Sacred offerings (zen 膳)**
These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== 参考文献 == * **"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * **"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] [[sn:Sarutahiko Shrine (Fukui)]]

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市) up to rev 95399178
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=猿田彦神社 |画像=[[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |所在地=[[福井県]][[福井市]]冬野町29-2 | 緯度度 =36 | 緯度分 =00 | 緯度秒 =47.23 | 経度度 =136 | 経度分 =11 | 経度秒 =13.73 |地図国コード = JP |祭神=[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]、白髭命、十一面観音菩薩 |神体= |社格= |創建=[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]年間 |本殿= （不明） |例祭=4月 |OSMサイズ = |OSMズーム = }} '''猿田彦神社'''（さるたひこじんじゃ）は、福井県[[福井市]]冬野町にある神社。
[[城山 (福井市)|城山（福井市）]]の麓。[[旧社格]]は村社。
== 歴史 == この神社は、[[廃仏毀釈]]以前には蕗野寺（吹野寺・冬野寺）と呼ばれる寺院であった。
神社に伝わる縁起額によると、[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]年間（701年 - 704年）に[[泰澄]]開基となった三番目の寺であるという<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.24</ref>。
== 境内 ==
* 雨乞いの池
== 祭神 ==
* [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] * 白髭命 * 十一面観音菩薩像（福井県指定の文化財）<ref>福井市の文化財 福井市教育委員会編 p.80</ref>
== 祭礼 == 祭礼は、毎年春に行われる。かつては、4月13日から4月14日にかけて行われていたが、現在はその日程に近い土日に行われる（2023年〈令和5年〉は4月8日、4月9日であった）。祭礼では、猿田彦神社から「ヤド（宿）」となる家へ、大きな[[御幣]]をつけた「オテングサン（お天狗さん）と言われる「天狗面」、「オシシサン（お獅子さん）」と呼ばれる「獅子頭」、御幣のつけられた鉾、御幣、膳が運ばれ、ヤドの床の間に置かれて一泊した後、戻る<ref>泰澄ゆかりの神仏 福井県立歴史博物館編 p.32−33</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references /> == 参考文献 ==
* 『泰澄ゆかりの神仏』福井県立歴史博物館編 2012年 * 『福井市の文化財』福井市教育委員会編 2021年
== 外部リンク ==
* [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 福井県神社庁]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さるたひこじんじゃ}} [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:福井市の建築物]] [[Category:福井市の歴史]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:8世紀設立]]

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva** (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a **Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture**.<ref>**"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The **annual festival** is held **in spring** every year. Previously, it was observed on **April 13–14**, but it is now celebrated **on the closest weekend** (for example, in **2023**, it was held on **April 8–9**).
During the festival, **sacred objects** are carried **from the shrine** to a designated **host home (Yado 宿)**. These include: - **"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)** – a **Tengu mask** (天狗面) - **"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)** – a **lion head** (獅子頭) - **A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)** - **Gohei (御幣)** - **Sacred offerings (zen 膳)**
These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>

'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the base of **{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}**. It was formerly classified as a **village shrine** (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a **Buddhist temple** known as **Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)** before the **Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation** (**{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft}}**).
According to the **enshrinement plaque** preserved at the shrine, it was the **third temple** founded by **{{ill|Taichō|ja|泰澄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}** during the **Taihō era** (701–704 CE).<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * **Rain-calling Pond** (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva** (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a **Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture**.<ref>**"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The **annual festival** is held **in spring** every year. Previously, it was observed on **April 13–14**, but it is now celebrated **on the closest weekend** (for example, in **2023**, it was held on **April 8–9**).
During the festival, **sacred objects** are carried **from the shrine** to a designated **host home (Yado 宿)**. These include: - **"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)** – a **Tengu mask** (天狗面) - **"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)** – a **lion head** (獅子頭) - **A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)** - **Gohei (御幣)** - **Sacred offerings (zen 膳)**
These objects are **enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight**, before being **returned to the shrine** the following day.<ref>**"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== 参考文献 == * **"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"**, Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * **"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"**, Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= |established= {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝 (日本)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= {{ill|April|ja|4月|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|ja|福井市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''{{ill|Taichō|ja|泰澄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== 参考文献 == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>

'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Fukui)|qq=}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''[[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]]''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''[[:en:Taichō|Taichō]]''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />

These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== 参考文献 == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= |established= [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō era]] (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= [[:en:April|April]] }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Fukui)|qq=}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''[[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]]''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''[[:en:Taichō|Taichō]]''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== 参考文献 == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= |established= [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō era]] (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= [[:en:April|April]] }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Fukui)|qq=}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''[[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]]''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''[[:en:Taichō|Taichō]]''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''[[:en:Taichō|Taichō]]''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.

It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).

According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''[[:en:Taichō|Taichō]]''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= |established= [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō era]] (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= [[:en:April|April]] }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Fukui)|qq=|1=Joyama (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117066803}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''[[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]]''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''[[:en:Taichō|Taichō]]''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= |established= [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō era]] (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= [[:en:April|April]] }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Fukui)|1=Joyama (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117066803}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''[[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]]''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''[[:en:Taichō|Taichō]]''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= |established= [[:en:Taihō (era)|Taihō era]] (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= [[:en:April|April]] }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui (city)]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]], at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Fukui)|1=Joyama (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117066803}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''[[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]]''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''[[:en:Taichō|Taichō]]''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
== External Links == * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20170112_113829 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]

According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''{{ill|Taichō|en|Taichō|WD=Q2388439|lt=Taichō}}''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>

'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui (city)}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture}}, at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Fukui)|1=Joyama (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117066803}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}}''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''{{ill|Taichō|en|Taichō|WD=Q2388439|lt=Taichō}}''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:猿田彦神社 (福井市)]]
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Sarutahiko Shrine |image= [[File:Entrance of Sarutahiko-jinja, Fukui.jpg|300px]] |location= {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui (city)|1=Fukui (city)}}, Fuyuno-chō 29-2 |coordinates= {{coord|36|00|47.23|N|136|11|13.73|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= |established= {{ill|Taihō (era)|en|Taihō (era)|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō era|1=Taihō (era)}} (701–704 CE) |main_hall= (Unknown) |annual_festival= {{ill|April|en|April|WD=Q118|lt=April|1=April}} }}
'''Sarutahiko Shrine''' (猿田彦神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui (city)|1=Fukui (city)}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}, at the base of '''{{ill|Shiroyama (Fukui)|ja|城山 (福井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama (Fukui)|1=Joyama (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117066803}}'''. It was formerly classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社).
== History == This shrine was originally a '''Buddhist temple''' known as '''Fukino-dera (also spelled Fuyuno-dera)''' before the '''Abolition of Buddhism and Shinto-Buddhism Separation''' ('''{{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}''').
According to the '''enshrinement plaque''' preserved at the shrine, it was the '''third temple''' founded by '''{{ill|Taichō|en|Taichō|WD=Q2388439|lt=Taichō|1=Taichō}}''' during the '''Taihō era''' (701–704 CE).<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.24</ref>
== Shrine facilities == * '''Rain-calling Pond''' (雨乞いの池)
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} * {{ill|Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja|白髭命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * '''Eleven-faced Kannon Bodhisattva''' (十一面観音菩薩) – Designated as a '''Cultural Property of Fukui Prefecture'''.<ref>'''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, p.80</ref>
== Festivals == The '''annual festival''' is held '''in spring''' every year. Previously, it was observed on '''April 13–14''', but it is now celebrated '''on the closest weekend''' (for example, in '''2023''', it was held on '''April 8–9''').
During the festival, '''sacred objects''' are carried '''from the shrine''' to a designated '''host home (Yado 宿)'''. These include: - '''"Oten'gu-san" (お天狗さん)''' – a '''Tengu mask''' (天狗面) - '''"Oshishi-san" (お獅子さん)''' – a '''lion head''' (獅子頭) - '''A ritual spear (Hoko 鉾)''' - '''Gohei (御幣)''' - '''Sacred offerings (zen 膳)'''
These objects are '''enshrined in the host home's tokonoma (alcove) overnight''', before being '''returned to the shrine''' the following day.<ref>'''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, p.32–33</ref>
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * '''"Taichō and His Associated Kami-Buddha Worship"''', Fukui Prefectural History Museum, 2012. * '''"Cultural Properties of Fukui City"''', Fukui City Board of Education, 2021.
